facts.text: Fort Meade began in 1849 as a military outpost during the Seminole Wars and was named for Army lieutenant George Meade, who later became a Civil War general.
A historic cannon on Fort Meade’s streets stands as a memorial honoring veterans and reflects the city’s long military and civic history.
The Fort Meade Public Library opened in 1934 when the Woman’s Club repurposed the abandoned Chamber of Commerce building at 75 East Broadway.
Farmers settled Fort Meade in the late 1800s, and many homes from that era still line its streets today.
Fort Meade Middle‑Senior High School sits near historic landmarks, including the Streamsong resort, and serves the town’s students.
The Florida Flywheelers grounds near Fort Meade host antique engine and tractor events that draw enthusiasts from across the region.
